title: FPGA Boards Catalog
description: Schemas for entities (standalone boards, SoMs, carriers, kits, FMC cards) and their relationships. Each entity type has its own folder under board-repo (boards/, soms/, carriers/, kits/, fmc-cards/); relationships live under relationships/<type>/. The build script validates every file in those folders against the matching $def.

standalone.title: Standalone Board
standalone.description: A complete FPGA development board with on-board user I/O — not a SoM, not part of a kit.

memory.title: Memory
memory.description: On-board DRAM. List one entry per distinct memory bank.

flash.title: Flash
flash.description: On-board non-volatile flash memory. List one entry per distinct chip.

usb.title: USB
usb.description: USB interfaces accessible to the FPGA design.

som.title: System-on-Module
som.description: A System-on-Module (SoM) — an FPGA + memory + flash on a small board designed to plug into a carrier via board-to-board mating connectors. Some SoMs include on-board debug peripherals (Ethernet, microSD, USB-UART) that are usable without a carrier.

memory.title: Memory
memory.description: On-SoM DRAM. List one entry per distinct memory bank.

usb.title: USB
usb.description: USB interfaces accessible to the FPGA design.

carrier.title: SoM Carrier
carrier.description: A carrier board that mates with one or more SoMs to provide user I/O. Has no FPGA of its own — the FPGA comes from the SoM. Compatibility with SoMs is recorded as som-mates relationships, not in the carrier file.

kit.title: Kit (SoM + Carrier)
kit.description: A bundled product comprising a SoM and a carrier (and possibly extras). All feature data is inherited from the composed entities at build time — kit files only contain identity, price, composition, and optional extras.

composition.title: Composition
composition.description: MPN references to the SoM and carrier that compose this kit. Both must exist as entities in soms/ and carriers/. The build script flattens the kit by merging SoM + carrier features into a single resolved record.

som.title: SoM MPN
som.description: MPN of the SoM in soms/<vendor>/<mpn>.json.

carrier.title: Carrier MPN
carrier.description: MPN of the carrier in carriers/<vendor>/<mpn>.json.

extras.title: Extras
extras.description: Additional items bundled with the kit beyond the SoM and carrier (cables, PSUs, antennas, etc.).

fmc_card.title: FMC Card
fmc_card.description: An FPGA Mezzanine Card (FMC LPC / HPC / FMC+) that plugs into an FMC site on a board or carrier. Compatibility is recorded as fmc-mates relationships, not in the card file. vadj range will live here once we model it — schema reserves the spot.

connector_type.title: FMC Connector Type
connector_type.description: LPC (160-pin VITA 57.1), HPC (400-pin VITA 57.1), or FMC+ (560-pin VITA 57.4).

vadj_min.title: VADJ Min (V)
vadj_min.description: Minimum VADJ supply voltage the card accepts. For fixed-voltage cards (e.g. 1.8V-only variants), set vadj_min == vadj_max.

vadj_max.title: VADJ Max (V)
vadj_max.description: Maximum VADJ supply voltage the card accepts.

som_mates.title: SoM Compatibility Bundle
som_mates.description: One file per carrier listing all SoMs that physically and electrically mate with it. Filename: relationships/som-mates/<carrier-vendor>/<carrier-mpn>.json — the parent folder matches the carrier's vendor (mirrors carriers/<vendor>/<mpn>.json), and the filename stem must match the top-level `carrier` field.

carrier.title: Carrier MPN
carrier.description: MPN of the carrier in carriers/<vendor>/<mpn>.json. Must match the filename.

som.title: SoM MPN
som.description: MPN of the SoM in soms/<vendor>/<mpn>.json.

fmc_mates.title: FMC Card Compatibility Bundle
fmc_mates.description: One file per FMC card listing all hosts (standalone boards, kits, or carriers) it mates with. Filename: relationships/fmc-mates/<fmc-card-vendor>/<fmc-card-mpn>.json — the parent folder matches the card's vendor (mirrors fmc-cards/<vendor>/<mpn>.json), and the filename stem must match the top-level `fmc_card` field.

fmc_card.title: FMC Card MPN
fmc_card.description: MPN of the FMC card in fmc-cards/<vendor>/<mpn>.json. Must match the filename.

host.title: Host MPN
host.description: MPN of the host (standalone board, kit, or carrier).

host_type.title: Host Type
host_type.description: Which entity folder the host lives in. Drives where the build script looks for the host file.

target_slot.title: Target Slot
target_slot.description: Name of the specific FMC slot on the host (matches a slot name in the host's fmc[] array). Omit if the relationship applies to any slot on the host. One entry per slot, so a host with multiple compatible sites gets multiple entries.

mpn.title: Part Number
mpn.description: Manufacturer part number (MPN / SKU). Must be unique within its entity type. URL-safe characters only (letters, digits, hyphens, dots, underscores). For non-buyable carriers (e.g. the KV260's carrier, which is only sold inside the kit), invent a stable MPN like <kit-mpn>-Carrier.

url.title: Product Page
url.description: Product page URL. For carriers that are not sold standalone, point at the parent kit's URL.

vendor.title: Vendor
vendor.description: Vendor key (must match a key in vendors.json board_vendors).

price_or_null.title: Price
price_or_null.description: Price. Use null when the price is unknown, not published, or the entity is not sold standalone.

verified_by.title: Verified By
verified_by.description: Who asserts this compatibility. 'vendor' = officially supported by the vendor. 'user' = community / user-reported. A future 'fpgadeveloper' value will indicate automated verification from vadj range + pin assignment data.

relationship_notes.title: Notes
relationship_notes.description: Free-text caveats specific to this combination (e.g. 'Only port 0 is usable on HPC1', 'Requires Vivado 2022.1+', 'Needs cooling modification').

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP).

vendor.description: Silicon vendor key (must match a key in vendors.json silicon_vendors).

type.description: Physical form factor of the PCIe interface. MCIO is the SFF-TA-1016 cabled PCIe connector (Mini Cool Edge IO).

ports.description: Number of ports at this speed.

usb_connector.description: Physical USB connector on the board.

speed.description: USB version (e.g. 2.0 = High-Speed 480 Mbps, 3.0 = SuperSpeed 5 Gbps).

role.description: host = peripherals plug in (Type-A), device = board acts as peripheral, otg = either.

ports.description: Number of physical ports of this kind. Defaults to 1 if omitted.

functions.description: What the bridge does — typically ["jtag", "uart"] for combined chips like FT2232.

ports.description: Number of physical ports of this kind. Defaults to 1 if omitted.

type.description: Flash family. QSPI/OSPI/SPI/BPI are NOR (typically used for FPGA configuration + some user data). eMMC is managed NAND for Linux rootfs storage. NAND is raw NAND.

size_mb.description: Total capacity of this chip in megabytes. 1 GB → 1024.

width_bits.description: Data bus width. Usually implicit from type (SPI=1, QSPI=4, OSPI=8). Useful to set for BPI (typically 16) and NAND (8 or 16).

type.description: DRAM family. HBM variants are on-package, not strictly DDR — listed here because they fill the same role in spec sheets.

size_mb.description: Total capacity of this bank in megabytes. 4 GB → 4096.

form_factor.description: Physical form. 'component' = soldered DRAM (default). Slot-based = sodimm / dimm / etc.

width_bits.description: Data bus width. 72 typically implies ECC.

ecc.description: True when this bank has ECC (error-correcting) support.

video.title: Video I/O
video.description: Video input/output interfaces. Only include keys with non-zero values.

networking.title: Networking
networking.description: All networking interfaces — Ethernet ports plus high-speed optical/copper cages.

ethernet.title: RJ45
ethernet.description: Wired Ethernet ports (RJ45 connectors), grouped by link speed. Separate from optical/copper SFP/QSFP cages which sit alongside this in the same `networking` object.

fmc_slot.title: FMC Slot
fmc_slot.description: A single FMC site on a host. vadj_min/vadj_max are reserved for future automated compatibility checking.

slot.title: Slot Name
slot.description: Name the vendor prints on the silkscreen (e.g. HPC0, HPC1, LPC, J3). Used by fmc-mates relationships to target a specific slot.

vadj_min.title: VADJ Min (V)
vadj_min.description: Minimum VADJ voltage supported by this slot. Reserved for future fpgadeveloper-validated compatibility.

vadj_max.title: VADJ Max (V)
vadj_max.description: Maximum VADJ voltage supported by this slot.

expansion.title: Expansion
expansion.description: Expansion connectors and headers. RFMC is kept as a count for now — it will be promoted to a richer first-class form once we model RFMC mezzanine cards.

fmc.title: FMC
fmc.description: FMC sites on the board/carrier/kit. One entry per slot. Each slot is a {slot, type} object — slot names appear on the silkscreen and are referenced by fmc-mates relationships.
